name: Further-Enhanced Type II Port Selection for Predicted PMI

lineage.adds: framing the exact same port-selection report as a prediction for one future slot interval

tagline: The same free-port-selection report as R17 — but explicitly labeled as a forecast for a moment slightly in the future, not a description of right now.
overview: A weather forecast for "tomorrow at noon" uses the same units and format as a report of "right now" — what changes is the intent: it is a prediction, not a snapshot. This codebook is exactly that relationship to R17's further-enhanced Type II port selection. It reuses the identical reconstruction formula, the identical free port-selection index, the identical tiny reciprocity-based tap window, and the identical amplitude/phase/bitmap machinery. The only difference is that the report is understood to describe **one future slot interval** rather than the present one — the single-interval special case ($N_4 = 1$) of the fuller Doppler-prediction machinery covered elsewhere in this catalog.

Because a moving phone's channel changes between the moment it measures CSI-RS (channel state information reference signal) and the moment the base station (**gNB**) actually transmits with the reported precoder, feedback inevitably describes a slightly stale channel — a problem known as **CSI aging**. Release 18 addresses this in two ways: a genuinely multi-interval Doppler-compressed report (covered by the eType II Doppler codebook in this catalog), and this simpler degenerate case, which keeps R17's cheap reciprocity-based report but frames it explicitly as a one-step-ahead prediction with its own configuration guards.

The parameter machinery — tap count $M$, port fraction $\alpha$, coefficient budget $\beta$ — and the amplitude/phase tables are all inherited unchanged from R17. What this codebook adds on top is a dedicated rank-restriction bitmap for the predicted-PMI context and an $R$ (subband-pairing) guard, both purely configuration-level additions; the reconstruction math and the UE-side selection algorithm are inherited verbatim.

This is a useful entry point for understanding what "predicted PMI" means at its simplest: nothing about the per-report math changes, only the interpretation of what moment in time the report describes and the accompanying scheduling guards around it.

howItWorks.title: UE measures the channel for a near-future interval
howItWorks.body: Rather than the current instant, the UE's measurement (or its prediction pipeline) targets one future slot interval that the report will describe when it is eventually used by the gNB.

howItWorks.title: UE freely selects the strongest ports
howItWorks.body: Exactly as in R17: any L of the P/2 ports per polarization are ranked by energy and selected via a general combinatorial index — no fixed stride.

howItWorks.title: UE finds at most one extra delay tap
howItWorks.body: The same reciprocity-based assumption applies: tap 0 is the reference, and only a tiny window is searched for a possible second tap.

howItWorks.title: UE quantizes amplitude, phase, and the coefficient bitmap
howItWorks.body: Identical R16/R17 quantization: per-polarization reference amplitude, per-coefficient differential amplitude, 16-PSK (phase-shift keying) phase, and a bitmap of which coefficients are kept.

howItWorks.title: Report travels over the uplink
howItWorks.body: The same PMI (precoding matrix indicator) fields as R17 are sent, now understood by the network to describe the predicted future interval rather than the present one.

howItWorks.title: gNB reconstructs for the predicted interval and transmits
howItWorks.body: The base station rebuilds the precoder exactly as in R17 and applies it when transmitting during the predicted interval, hopefully closer to the true channel than a now-stale earlier report would have been.

whatIsReported.plain: Which L ports (out of P/2) were freely selected, per polarization.
whatIsReported.detail: Identical to R17: a general combinatorial index over any port subset, reported only when alpha < 1.

whatIsReported.plain: The offset of the second delay tap, if used.
whatIsReported.detail: Identical to R17: only reported when M=2 and the window is wide enough to need an explicit offset.

whatIsReported.plain: A bitmap marking which port/tap coefficients are nonzero, per layer.
whatIsReported.detail: Identical mechanism to R17, now covering the coefficients that describe the predicted interval.

whatIsReported.plain: Which coefficient is the strongest, per layer.
whatIsReported.detail: Identical dual-mode strongest-coefficient indicator inherited from R16/R17.

whatIsReported.plain: The per-polarization reference amplitude, per layer.
whatIsReported.detail: Identical to R17's 4-bit-per-polarization reference amplitude mechanism.

parametersExplained.plain: The same (M, alpha, beta) preset table as R17.
parametersExplained.guidance: Choose exactly as you would for R17 — the parameter semantics are unchanged.

parametersExplained.plain: The size of the second-tap search window, when M=2.

strengths: Directly targets CSI aging with essentially no extra reporting cost over R17.
Inherits R17's free port selection and tiny reciprocity tap window unchanged.
Simple mental model: it is R17, relabeled for a one-step-ahead prediction.
A natural stepping stone to the fuller multi-interval Doppler-compressed prediction codebook.
limitations: Predicts only a single future interval — it has no temporal (Doppler) compression axis of its own.
Prediction quality depends entirely on how good the UE's forecast of the future channel is; the codebook itself assumes that forecast is available.
Inherits every R17 limitation: TDD reciprocity assumption, no DFT beam search, R not applied to reconstruction.
The exact slot-interval timing and scheduling machinery around when the prediction applies is not modeled by the codebook math itself.
whenToUse: Use this codebook to explore the cheapest form of predicted CSI feedback — when a single future interval's prediction is enough and you want R17's low overhead rather than the full multi-interval Doppler codebook's larger report.

mathHighlight.caption: The reconstruction formula is identical to R17's; only the temporal interpretation of the report differs.
